definition: A title used for the Buddha, often carrying doctrinal weight that should not be flattened into a casual English equivalent.
notes: The project leaves Tathāgata untranslated because attempts to fully render it in English are unstable and usually distract from the doctrinal use of the title. A short gloss is acceptable on first occurrence when needed. The title often carries special doctrinal weight in statements about the Buddha, so the project avoids forcing a supposedly definitive English solution. The OSF glossary likewise keeps Tathāgata untranslated as a technical title, which supports the present handling.

context_rules.context: In most source-facing and doctrinal contexts
context_rules.notes: Leave the title untranslated by default.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a gloss is necessary
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only as a gloss.

context_rules.context: When the passage uses the title in doctrinally self-referential Buddha-speech
context_rules.notes: Keep the title explicit to preserve its technical and rhetorical force.

example_phrases.notes: Common titular usage throughout the Nikāyas.
Shows that the title often appears in highly doctrinal compounds.

authority_basis.scope: Named in notes as support for the current Tathāgata policy.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'Tathāgata' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not force an English substitute when the local passage needs the Pali term; use a gloss only when the entry or passage explicitly calls for one.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'messiah' or other unrecorded alternates.
translation_policy.leave_untranslated_when: Leave the term untranslated when source-facing doctrinal range or local explanation would be narrowed too quickly by an English equivalent.
